steps.title: Spot the passage's key condition
steps.content: The passage establishes that difrasismo meanings seem obscure because semantic relations are “tenuous,” yet they become intelligible through “conceptual associations observed in Aztec ceremonial culture.” The decisive clue: obscurity is resolved by proper cultural context.

steps.title: Match this condition to the choices
steps.content:
C states exactly this—apparent obscurity resolves when considered in proper cultural context, which mirrors the passage's account of how difrasismos work. The other three either introduce unsupported claims (A about frequency, B about modern readers and formal function), or contradict the passage (D claims semantic equivalence when relations are explicitly tenuous).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
